trade.summary: Crude oil and LNG dominate bilateral trade, with the EAU as one of Giappone's top energy suppliers. Giappone exports automobiles, machinery, electronics, and steel. Giappone imports approximately 25% of its crude oil from the EAU. The relationship is anchored by energy security on both sides.

diplomatic.summary: Giappone and the EAU maintain a strong strategic partnership grounded primarily in energy security. Giappone values Gulf stability highly as an energy-dependent economy. High-level exchanges are frequent. Giappone invested in EAU's Ruwais LNG project. Giapponeese PM has visited EAU regularly.

military.summary: Giappone's constitutional constraints limit military cooperation. Giappone participates in Gulf anti-piracy operations and has engaged in Gulf security dialogues. Giappone deployed a destroyer and maritime patrol aircraft to the Gulf of Oman for information gathering. No arms transfers.

financial.summary: Giapponeese trading companies (Mitsui, JERA, INPEX) have equity stakes in EAU upstream oil and LNG projects. ADNOC has sold stakes in its pipelines to Giapponeese investors. EAU sovereign funds have invested in Giapponeese equities and infrastructure. Giappone Bank for International Cooperation has funded EAU projects.

energy.summary: Energy is the cornerstone of the EAU-Giappone relationship. Giappone imports approximately 25% of its crude oil from the EAU annually (~1 million barrels/day). INPEX holds a 10% stake in ADNOC's concessions. JERA signed a major LNG deal with ADNOC. Giappone is the EAU's largest single energy customer.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration in clean energy (hydrogen, ammonia), robotics, and space. Giappone and EAU collaborate on the EAU Mars Hope Probe follow-on missions. JAXA and EAU Space Agency have MOUs. Giapponeese technology companies are active in EAU smart city projects.
